food_subgroup: Kopitiam set meal — toast with spread and hot beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Singaporean kopitiam toast-and-beverage set meal. FOODON_00002473 (meal food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00EJ (composite/mixed dishes) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a Singaporean kopitiam set meal with toast and hot beverage.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to a Singaporean kopitiam toast-and-beverage set meal.

main_ingredients: hot beverage (coffee or tea, medium size)

serving.description: 1 set (2 slices toast with raspberry-vanilla spread + 1 medium hot beverage ~250 ml)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Toast Box set comprising two slices of toasted white bread with raspberry-vanilla spread and butter, served alongside one medium-size hot beverage (approximately 250 ml coffee or tea), totalling approximately 320 g.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database — White bread (toasted), Kaya / jam spread, Butter; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Raspberry jam, Vanilla flavouring; HPB Foodo — Kopi/Teh (hot coffee/tea with condensed milk)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Toast Box menu set. Nutrients estimated by component-level analysis: (1) 2 slices toasted white bread (~60 g) from HPB Singapore nutrient database; (2) raspberry-vanilla spread and butter (~30 g combined) estimated from HPB kaya-toast spread data and USDA raspberry jam SR Legacy entry; (3) medium hot beverage with condensed milk (~230 ml, ~30 g condensed milk equivalent) from HPB Foodo kopi/teh data. Values are blended per 100 g of the total set weight (~320 g). Confidence is medium due to component estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread and jam-spread toast meals — analogous estimate based on: 'International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008' (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care, 2008); University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread (GI ~70) and jam (GI ~51)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Toast Box raspberry-vanilla set. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on: white bread GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database), raspberry jam GI ~51 (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care), condensed-milk hot beverage GI ~61 (analogous to sweetened milky coffee/tea). Weighted blend across set components yields an estimated composite GI of ~65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 100.5) / 100 ≈ 65.3 g. Carbohydrate per serving = 31.5 g/100g × 320 g = 100.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from bread, butter, and condensed-milk beverage
hypertension.guidance: Each set provides approximately 474 mg sodium per serving; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one set per occasion and choose lower-sodium beverage options such as plain tea without condensed milk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar load from white bread, raspberry jam, and sweetened condensed-milk beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: The set carries an estimated glycemic load of ~65 per serving driven by white bread (GI ~70) and sweetened spread and beverage; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider wholemeal bread substitution, reduced-sugar spread, and unsweetened tea or coffee to substantially lower postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter and condensed milk, and added sugar from jam and sweetened beverage
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The set contributes approximately 12.5 g saturated fat and 45 g added sugar per serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and request reduced butter and unsweetened beverage to lower saturated fat and sugar intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Raspberry Vanilla Set with Medium-Size Hot Beverage is a popular Singaporean kopitiam-style set meal offered by Toast Box, a well-known local toast café chain. The set typically comprises two slices of toasted white bread generously spread with a raspberry-vanilla flavoured jam or cream spread and a layer of salted butter, served alongside one medium-size hot beverage — most commonly kopi (Singaporean-style coffee brewed with a sock filter and served with sweetened condensed milk) or teh (milk tea with condensed milk). The toast is crisp on the outside and soft inside, delivering a sweet-fruity flavour from the raspberry jam balanced by the creamy vanilla note and the richness of butter. The hot beverage adds a bittersweet, milky depth characteristic of traditional kopitiam drinks. Nutritionally, the set is energy-dense at approximately 634 kcal per serving, with a high carbohydrate load (~101 g) dominated by refined white bread and added sugars from the jam and condensed milk. The estimated composite glycemic index of ~65 (medium) and glycemic load of ~65 per serving make this a high-glycaemic meal that warrants caution for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. Saturated fat (~12.5 g per serving) from butter and condensed milk is relevant for cardiovascular risk management. Sodium (~474 mg per serving) is moderate and relevant for hypertension. The set is a culturally significant breakfast or teatime staple in Singapore, reflecting the kopitiam tradition of pairing toast with a hot local beverage. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal. Wholemeal bread substitution, reduced-sugar spread, and unsweetened beverage options are available at many Toast Box outlets and are recommended for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Toast Box menu item. Nutrient values are estimated by component-level analysis using HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for individual ingredients (toasted white bread, raspberry jam, vanilla spread, butter, condensed-milk hot beverage). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). Confidence is medium because the component blend is defensible but not directly measured.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated for a 320 g set (2 slices toasted white bread ~60 g + spread/butter ~30 g + medium hot beverage ~230 g). Per-100 g values are derived from this blend. 2. Vitamin B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, and selenium are reported as -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo entries for the component foods used in this estimation, and cannot be reliably imputed without a direct source. 3. GI set to 65 (medium) as an analogous estimate; glycemic load per serving ~65.3 based on ~100.8 g carbohydrate per 320 g serving. 4. The hot beverage component is assumed to be kopi or teh with condensed milk (standard Toast Box default); nutritional values will differ if the customer selects a sugar-free or black beverage option. 5. Allergen profile assumes standard Toast Box preparation; cross-contamination risk exists in shared kitchen environments. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from component data and carry moderate uncertainty. 7. Vitamin B6 is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used. Vitamin B12 is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used. Folate is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component entries used.
